vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-22259
vulnerability.description: Spring Framework URL parsing open redirect / SSRF via UriComponentsBuilder

statements.impact_statement: The payment-service and order-service use UriComponentsBuilder to parse callback URLs from payment gateway webhooks. The user-service uses it to validate OAuth2 redirect URIs. All three services are vulnerable to open redirect attacks that could be chained with SSRF to access AWS metadata service (169.254.169.254). PCI cardholder data is at risk.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade Spring Framework to 6.1.5 or later. As an immediate mitigation, add an allowlist of permitted redirect hosts in the webhook callback handler. Apply network-level protection by configuring the EC2 instance metadata service to require IMDSv2 (hop limit = 1).

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-22243
vulnerability.description: Spring Framework URL parsing open redirect / SSRF via UriComponentsBuilder

statements.impact_statement: Same attack surface as CVE-2024-22259. The URL parsing flaw exists in the same UriComponentsBuilder class. Our services parse externally-provided redirect URLs in the OAuth2 flow and payment gateway callback handlers.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade Spring Framework to 6.1.4 or later. This is addressed by the same upgrade that fixes CVE-2024-22259.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2022-42003
vulnerability.description: jackson-databind DoS via deep wrapper array nesting with UNWRAP_SINGLE_VALUE_ARRAYS

statements.impact_statement: Our deployment uses jackson-databind 2.16.1 which includes the fix for CVE-2022-42003 (fixed in 2.13.4.1). The version in our SBOM is post-fix. Additionally, none of our ObjectMapper configurations enable UNWRAP_SINGLE_VALUE_ARRAYS, which is required to trigger the vulnerability.
statements.action_statement: No action required. Vulnerability is patched in the deployed version. Maintain current version or newer.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-35116
vulnerability.description: jackson-databind DoS via crafted object with cyclic dependencies

statements.impact_statement: The vendor (FasterXML) disputes this CVE, noting that constructing cyclic dependencies requires application-level logic and is not achievable through deserialization alone. Our services deserialize JSON from HTTP request bodies; attackers cannot construct Java object graphs with cyclic references via JSON input.
statements.action_statement: No action required. The reported vulnerability requires application-level cooperation that is not present in our code. Continue monitoring FasterXML advisories.

statements.impact_statement: tough-cookie is a transitive dependency of axios used in the API gateway and frontend build. The API gateway uses axios for outbound HTTP requests to internal microservices. We need to determine whether CookieJar is instantiated with rejectPublicSuffixes=false in any code path. Preliminary analysis suggests our usage does not trigger the vulnerable code path, but a full audit of transitive usage patterns is pending.
statements.action_statement: Investigation in progress. Audit all axios usage in api-gateway and notification-service for CookieJar instantiation. Update tough-cookie to 4.1.3+ regardless of exploitability to eliminate the dependency risk. Target completion: 2026-03-05.

statements.impact_statement: @babel/traverse is a build-time devDependency used only during the frontend compilation step in the CI/CD pipeline. It is not included in the production React bundle deployed to CloudFront. The vulnerability requires an attacker to control input to the Babel compiler, which is not possible in our build environment. The compiled production JavaScript does not contain @babel/traverse.
statements.action_statement: No action required for production. Update @babel/traverse to 7.23.2+ in devDependencies to eliminate risk during CI/CD builds. Low priority.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-21538
vulnerability.description: cross-spawn Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)

statements.impact_statement: cross-spawn is a build-time dependency used by npm scripts and build tooling. It is not required at runtime by express or any of our production Node.js services. The ReDoS vulnerability requires crafted command-line arguments, which are not controllable by external users in our production deployment.
statements.action_statement: No action required for production. Update cross-spawn to 7.0.5+ in devDependencies. Low priority.

statements.impact_statement: The payment-service uses the cryptography library for TLS certificate management and PKCS12 operations when rotating mTLS certificates with the payment gateway (Stripe). A malformed PKCS12 file could crash the certificate rotation process. The risk is partially mitigated because PKCS12 files are generated internally, not from user input. However, a compromised internal PKI or supply chain attack on certificate bundles could trigger this.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade cryptography to 42.0.4 or later. Add input validation for PKCS12 file format before processing. Implement crash recovery in the certificate rotation job.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-26130
vulnerability.description: cryptography NULL pointer dereference in pkcs12.serialize_key_and_certificates

statements.impact_statement: The auth-service calls pkcs12.serialize_key_and_certificates when generating client certificates for mTLS authentication. If the certificate public key does not match the private key and hmac_hash is None, the process crashes. This is exploitable if an attacker can influence the certificate or key material provided to the serialization function. The crash leads to denial of service of the authentication service.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade cryptography to 42.0.4 or later. Add explicit key-pair validation before calling serialize_key_and_certificates. Always specify hmac_hash parameter explicitly.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-50782
vulnerability.description: cryptography Bleichenbacher timing oracle against RSA decryption

statements.impact_statement: The payment-service uses RSA key exchanges for encrypting cardholder data in transit to the tokenization service. A sophisticated attacker with the ability to capture TLS traffic and send millions of modified ciphertexts could potentially decrypt captured payment data using a Bleichenbacher timing attack. This requires sustained network access to the internal service mesh and significant computational resources. The attack complexity is HIGH but the data sensitivity is CRITICAL (PCI cardholder data).
statements.action_statement: Upgrade cryptography to 42.0.0 or later (fixed in this version; verify our 42.0.2 includes the patch). Migrate RSA key exchanges to ECDHE where possible. Ensure TLS 1.3 is enforced on all internal service-to-service communication, which eliminates RSA key exchange entirely. Enable PFS (Perfect Forward Secrecy) on all TLS configurations.
